postgres-dba.title: PostgreSQL Fundamentals
postgres-dba.description: Learn the core concepts of relational databases using PostgreSQL as the engine. Understand how to install, configure, and interact with a PostgreSQL server.
postgres-dba.challenge: Create a local PostgreSQL instance, create a database, define three related tables, and perform basic CRUD operations using psql.

postgres-dba.title: SQL Querying and Joins
postgres-dba.description: Master advanced SQL queries including joins, subqueries, aggregations, and window functions. Practice writing efficient queries against multi-table schemas.
postgres-dba.challenge: Write 10 progressively complex queries against a sample e-commerce schema including at least one CTE and one window function.

postgres-dba.title: Indexing and Query Optimization
postgres-dba.description: Understand how PostgreSQL uses indexes to speed up queries and learn when and how to create the right index type. Analyze query plans to identify bottlenecks.
postgres-dba.challenge: Take a slow query from a test dataset, analyze it with EXPLAIN ANALYZE, add appropriate indexes, and document the performance improvement.

postgres-dba.title: Transactions, Locking, and Concurrency
postgres-dba.description: Learn how PostgreSQL handles concurrent access through MVCC and locking mechanisms. Understand isolation levels and how to prevent deadlocks.
postgres-dba.challenge: Simulate a deadlock scenario in a test environment, identify it using pg_locks, and refactor the transaction order to eliminate the deadlock.

postgres-dba.title: Backup, Restore, and PITR
postgres-dba.description: Learn backup strategies for PostgreSQL including logical and physical backups and point-in-time recovery. Practice restore procedures to verify backup integrity.
postgres-dba.challenge: Set up WAL archiving, simulate data loss, and restore the database to a specific point in time using PITR.

postgres-dba.title: Replication Fundamentals
postgres-dba.description: Configure streaming replication between a primary and one or more standby servers. Understand synchronous vs asynchronous replication trade-offs.
postgres-dba.challenge: Configure a primary and standby PostgreSQL server with streaming replication and verify that changes on primary propagate to standby.

postgres-dba.title: PostgreSQL Security and Access Control
postgres-dba.description: Implement security best practices including role-based access control, SSL encryption, and network-level restrictions. Audit access and privilege assignments.
postgres-dba.challenge: Create a multi-role access control scheme with read-only, read-write, and admin roles, enable SSL, and test that privileges are enforced correctly.

postgres-dba.title: Performance Tuning and Configuration
postgres-dba.description: Tune PostgreSQL configuration parameters for memory, connections, and I/O to maximize performance for a specific workload. Use pg_stat_statements to identify slow queries.
postgres-dba.challenge: Profile a write-heavy workload, tune postgresql.conf for that workload, and show measurable throughput improvement using pgbench.

postgres-dba.title: Logical Replication, Partitioning, and Extensions
postgres-dba.description: Use logical replication for cross-version migrations and selective table replication. Implement table partitioning for large datasets and explore useful extensions.
postgres-dba.challenge: Set up logical replication from one database to another, partition a 10-million-row table by range, and query it to verify partition pruning.

postgres-dba.title: High Availability, Patroni, and Production Operations
postgres-dba.description: Deploy a production-grade highly available PostgreSQL cluster using Patroni for automatic failover and etcd for consensus. Implement automated backup with pgBackRest.
postgres-dba.challenge: Deploy a 3-node Patroni cluster, simulate a primary failure, verify automatic failover, and restore from a pgBackRest backup to a point in time.

mysql-dba.title: MySQL Fundamentals
mysql-dba.description: Get started with MySQL by installing the server and learning to interact with it via the command-line client and MySQL Workbench. Understand the basic data types and SQL syntax.
mysql-dba.challenge: Install MySQL, create a schema for a library system with books, authors, and members tables, and populate it with sample data.

mysql-dba.title: InnoDB Storage Engine
mysql-dba.description: Understand the InnoDB storage engine, which provides ACID-compliant transactions and row-level locking. Learn about its internal architecture including buffer pool and redo logs.
mysql-dba.challenge: Configure InnoDB buffer pool size, create a transactional test scenario, and verify that uncommitted data is isolated from other sessions.

mysql-dba.title: Indexing and Query Optimization
mysql-dba.description: Learn to create and manage indexes in MySQL and use EXPLAIN to analyze query execution plans. Enable and interpret the slow query log to find problem queries.
mysql-dba.challenge: Enable the slow query log, identify the top 5 slowest queries in a test workload, and optimize each with appropriate indexes or query rewrites.

mysql-dba.title: Backup and Recovery
mysql-dba.description: Implement a comprehensive backup strategy using logical and physical backup tools. Practice full and incremental restores including binary log-based point-in-time recovery.
mysql-dba.challenge: Perform a full backup with XtraBackup, apply binary logs to restore to a specific transaction, and verify data consistency after recovery.

mysql-dba.title: User Management and Security
mysql-dba.description: Secure MySQL by configuring user accounts, privileges, and encrypted connections. Understand the privilege system and best practices for least-privilege access.
mysql-dba.challenge: Create roles for read-only analyst, read-write app user, and DBA, assign users to roles, enable SSL, and test that each role has only the intended access.

mysql-dba.title: MySQL Replication Basics
mysql-dba.description: Configure MySQL asynchronous replication with GTID-based auto-positioning. Understand the binary log format and how replication threads work.
mysql-dba.challenge: Set up a GTID-based primary-replica pair, stop the replica, make changes on primary, restart replica, and verify it catches up automatically.

mysql-dba.title: Performance Schema and Monitoring
mysql-dba.description: Use the Performance Schema and sys schema to monitor query performance, wait events, and resource usage in real time. Set up external monitoring with Prometheus and mysqld_exporter.
mysql-dba.challenge: Use the sys schema to identify the top 10 queries by total latency, then set up Prometheus and Grafana to visualize MySQL metrics in a dashboard.

mysql-dba.title: MySQL Configuration Tuning
mysql-dba.description: Tune MySQL configuration for production workloads by adjusting memory, I/O, and connection settings. Understand how to benchmark changes using sysbench.
mysql-dba.challenge: Run a sysbench OLTP benchmark before and after tuning InnoDB buffer pool and log file size, and document the TPS improvement.

mysql-dba.title: Group Replication and InnoDB Cluster
mysql-dba.description: Configure MySQL Group Replication for multi-primary or single-primary fault-tolerant setups. Use MySQL Shell and MySQL Router to build a full InnoDB Cluster.
mysql-dba.challenge: Deploy a 3-node InnoDB Cluster using MySQL Shell, simulate a primary failure, and verify automatic failover with MySQL Router re-routing connections.

mysql-dba.title: Production Operations and Disaster Recovery
mysql-dba.description: Operate MySQL at production scale using ProxySQL for connection pooling and query routing, and Orchestrator for topology management and automated failover. Implement a full DR plan.
mysql-dba.challenge: Set up ProxySQL in front of a replicated MySQL cluster, configure read-write splitting rules, trigger a failover via Orchestrator, and verify zero query routing errors.

mongodb-admin.title: MongoDB Fundamentals
mongodb-admin.description: Learn the document model of MongoDB and how it differs from relational databases. Install MongoDB, use mongosh, and perform basic CRUD operations on collections.
mongodb-admin.challenge: Create a MongoDB database for a blog platform with posts, comments, and users collections, and perform all CRUD operations using mongosh.

mongodb-admin.title: Querying and Aggregation
mongodb-admin.description: Master MongoDB Query Language (MQL) for filtering documents and learn the aggregation pipeline for complex data transformations and analytics.
mongodb-admin.challenge: Write an aggregation pipeline that calculates total sales by category, filters categories with more than 100 sales, and sorts the result by revenue.

mongodb-admin.title: Indexing in MongoDB
mongodb-admin.description: Create and manage indexes in MongoDB to improve query performance. Use explain() to analyze query plans and understand when indexes are and are not used.
mongodb-admin.challenge: Identify a collection scan in a slow query using explain(), add appropriate indexes, and demonstrate a covered query that returns data from the index alone.

mongodb-admin.title: Schema Design and Data Modeling
mongodb-admin.description: Design MongoDB schemas by choosing between embedding and referencing documents based on access patterns. Apply design patterns like bucket, polymorphic, and outlier.
mongodb-admin.challenge: Design a schema for an IoT sensor platform storing time-series readings, apply the bucket pattern to reduce document count, and compare query performance.

mongodb-admin.title: Backup and Security
mongodb-admin.description: Implement backup and restore procedures using mongodump and mongorestore or Atlas Backup. Secure MongoDB with role-based access control and TLS encryption.
mongodb-admin.challenge: Enable RBAC with least-privilege roles, configure TLS for client connections, perform a full mongodump backup, drop a collection, and restore it.

mongodb-admin.title: Replica Sets
mongodb-admin.description: Deploy a MongoDB replica set for high availability and data redundancy. Understand the oplog, election process, and read preference options for distributing load.
mongodb-admin.challenge: Deploy a 3-member replica set, step down the primary, observe the election, and configure an application to route reads to secondary nodes.

mongodb-admin.title: Sharding and Horizontal Scaling
mongodb-admin.description: Scale MongoDB horizontally by configuring a sharded cluster with config servers and mongos routers. Choose shard keys strategically to avoid hotspots.
mongodb-admin.challenge: Deploy a 2-shard cluster with config servers and mongos, enable sharding on a collection with a hashed shard key, and verify even chunk distribution.

mongodb-admin.title: Performance Monitoring and Tuning
mongodb-admin.description: Monitor MongoDB performance using built-in tools and Atlas dashboards. Identify slow operations, analyze the profiler output, and tune configuration parameters.
mongodb-admin.challenge: Enable the database profiler at level 1, load test the cluster, identify the top 3 slow operations, and optimize them with indexes or query rewrites.

mongodb-admin.title: Transactions and Change Streams
mongodb-admin.description: Use multi-document ACID transactions for operations that span multiple collections. Leverage change streams to build real-time event-driven applications.
mongodb-admin.challenge: Implement a fund transfer between two account documents using a multi-document transaction and build a change stream listener that logs all balance changes.

mongodb-admin.title: Atlas Operations and Production Readiness
mongodb-admin.description: Operate MongoDB at production scale using Atlas managed service, including cluster tier selection, automated backups, Atlas Search, and global distributed clusters.
mongodb-admin.challenge: Deploy a production-like Atlas cluster with a 3-region global setup, configure Atlas Search on a text field, set up backup policies, and simulate a region failure.

data-modeler.title: Relational Data Modeling Basics
data-modeler.description: Learn the fundamentals of relational data modeling including entities, attributes, and relationships. Draw entity-relationship diagrams and translate them to SQL tables.
data-modeler.challenge: Model a university registration system with students, courses, instructors, and enrollments, draw the ERD, and write the DDL SQL for all tables.

data-modeler.title: Normalization Forms
data-modeler.description: Understand the first through third normal forms and how to apply normalization to remove data redundancy and update anomalies. Practice decomposing denormalized tables.
data-modeler.challenge: Take a single unnormalized spreadsheet of invoice data and normalize it to 3NF, documenting each step and the functional dependencies identified.

data-modeler.title: Advanced ER Modeling
data-modeler.description: Apply advanced ER modeling concepts including weak entities, generalization, and specialization hierarchies. Use Crow's Foot and UML notations in professional tools.
data-modeler.challenge: Model a healthcare system with patients, doctors, specialists, appointments, and diagnoses using ISA hierarchies and draw it in Crow's Foot notation.

data-modeler.title: Dimensional Modeling for Analytics
data-modeler.description: Design data warehouse schemas using dimensional modeling techniques. Understand fact and dimension tables and choose between star and snowflake schemas.
data-modeler.challenge: Design a star schema for an e-commerce analytics system with sales facts and date, product, customer, and geography dimensions.

data-modeler.title: SQL DDL and Schema Implementation
data-modeler.description: Translate data models into production SQL schemas using proper DDL with constraints, defaults, and domain types. Manage schema changes safely.
data-modeler.challenge: Implement the healthcare system model from earlier as a complete PostgreSQL schema with all constraints, and write a migration script to add a new diagnosis_notes column.

data-modeler.title: NoSQL and Polyglot Data Modeling
data-modeler.description: Learn how data modeling differs for document, key-value, and wide-column stores. Design schemas driven by access patterns rather than normalization rules.
data-modeler.challenge: Design a DynamoDB single-table schema for a social media platform supporting user lookups, post feeds, and comment threads with minimal queries.

data-modeler.title: Data Integrity and Referential Integrity
data-modeler.description: Enforce data integrity at the database level using foreign key constraints, check constraints, and triggers. Understand cascading actions and deferrable constraints.
data-modeler.challenge: Design a bank account schema with triggers that prevent negative balances and foreign key constraints that cascade correctly when accounts are closed.

data-modeler.title: Temporal and Historical Data Modeling
data-modeler.description: Model data that changes over time using temporal tables and bitemporal design. Track both valid time and transaction time for full audit history.
topics: Event sourcing as a data model

resources.label: Temporal Data and the Relational Model

data-modeler.challenge: Add bitemporal tracking to the bank account schema, storing both when a balance was valid and when each change was recorded.

data-modeler.title: Schema Migration and Evolution
data-modeler.description: Manage schema changes in a team environment using migration tools. Design schemas that can evolve safely without downtime using expand-contract patterns.
data-modeler.challenge: Use Flyway to manage a sequence of 5 schema migrations on a test database, including one zero-downtime column rename using the expand-contract pattern.

data-modeler.title: Enterprise Data Architecture and Governance
data-modeler.description: Apply data modeling at enterprise scale with canonical data models, master data management, and data governance practices. Document and govern schemas using data catalogs.
data-modeler.challenge: Design a canonical data model for a retail enterprise, document it in a data catalog tool, and define data lineage from source systems to analytics tables.

nosql-engineer.title: NoSQL Foundations
nosql-engineer.description: Understand the motivations for NoSQL databases and the theoretical foundations including CAP theorem and BASE consistency. Survey the main NoSQL categories.
nosql-engineer.challenge: Research and compare MongoDB, Cassandra, Redis, and DynamoDB across five dimensions (consistency model, scaling, query model, latency, use cases) in a written analysis.

nosql-engineer.title: MongoDB for Application Development
nosql-engineer.description: Build applications backed by MongoDB, designing schemas around access patterns and using the aggregation pipeline for analytics. Integrate with Node.js using Mongoose.
nosql-engineer.challenge: Build a REST API backed by MongoDB that supports paginated listing, text search, and an aggregation endpoint reporting totals by category.

nosql-engineer.title: Redis for Caching and Data Structures
nosql-engineer.description: Use Redis as a cache and in-memory data store, leveraging its rich data structure types. Implement caching patterns like cache-aside and write-through.
topics: Redis data types (strings, lists, sets, hashes, sorted sets)
nosql-engineer.challenge: Add Redis caching to a Node.js API using cache-aside pattern, implement a leaderboard using sorted sets, and benchmark the API with and without cache.

nosql-engineer.title: Apache Cassandra Basics
nosql-engineer.description: Get started with Apache Cassandra, understanding its distributed architecture and query-first data modeling approach. Write CQL queries and design tables around known access patterns.
nosql-engineer.challenge: Design and implement a Cassandra schema for a time-series event log that supports efficient queries by user ID and time range.

nosql-engineer.title: DynamoDB Design and Operations
nosql-engineer.description: Design DynamoDB tables using single-table design principles for efficiency and scalability. Use Global Secondary Indexes to support multiple access patterns.
nosql-engineer.challenge: Design a DynamoDB single-table schema for an order management system that supports lookups by order ID, customer orders list, and orders by status.

nosql-engineer.title: Redis Advanced Patterns
nosql-engineer.description: Use advanced Redis features including Pub/Sub messaging, Redis Streams for event processing, and RedisSearch for full-text search capabilities.
nosql-engineer.challenge: Implement a real-time activity feed using Redis Streams with consumer groups, and add a distributed rate limiter for an API endpoint.

nosql-engineer.title: Cassandra Data Modeling Patterns
nosql-engineer.description: Apply advanced Cassandra data modeling patterns including time-series, user activity, and time-bucket patterns. Handle multi-access-pattern requirements with denormalization.
nosql-engineer.challenge: Re-design the event log schema from the earlier layer to handle a traffic spike scenario using time-bucket partitioning and test it with a load test.

nosql-engineer.title: Multi-model and Polyglot Persistence
nosql-engineer.description: Design a polyglot persistence architecture where different data stores serve different components based on their strengths. Manage consistency across multiple stores.
nosql-engineer.challenge: Design an e-commerce system using PostgreSQL for orders, Redis for cart and sessions, and Elasticsearch for product search, with an event-driven sync layer.

nosql-engineer.title: Observability and Performance Tuning
nosql-engineer.description: Monitor and tune multiple NoSQL systems using metrics, tracing, and profiling tools. Set up dashboards and alerts for key performance indicators.
topics: Redis INFO command and latency tracking
nosql-engineer.challenge: Set up Prometheus exporters and Grafana dashboards for MongoDB, Redis, and Cassandra running locally, and create alerts for high latency and error rate.

nosql-engineer.title: Production Deployment and Resilience
nosql-engineer.description: Deploy NoSQL systems to Kubernetes using Helm charts and manage infrastructure with Terraform. Apply chaos engineering to test resilience under failure conditions.
topics: Helm charts for MongoDB, Redis, Cassandra
nosql-engineer.challenge: Deploy a MongoDB replica set and Redis cluster on Kubernetes using Helm, inject pod failures with a chaos tool, and verify the system recovers automatically.

db-reliability.title: Database Reliability Fundamentals
db-reliability.description: Understand SRE principles applied to database systems, including SLOs, error budgets, and the reliability mindset. Learn how databases fail and how to reason about reliability.
db-reliability.challenge: Define SLOs for a hypothetical production database, including availability, query latency p99, and replication lag, with justification for each target.

db-reliability.title: Monitoring and Alerting
db-reliability.description: Set up a full monitoring stack for database systems using Prometheus, Grafana, and exporters. Create actionable alerts based on golden signals.
db-reliability.challenge: Deploy Prometheus with postgresql_exporter and mysqld_exporter, create Grafana dashboards for each, and write alert rules for connection saturation and replication lag.

db-reliability.title: Backup Strategies and Validation
db-reliability.description: Design and implement comprehensive backup strategies for multiple database systems. Validate backups regularly by performing test restores in isolated environments.
db-reliability.challenge: Automate weekly full and nightly incremental backups for a PostgreSQL instance, run a weekly restore test in a separate environment, and alert on restore failure.

db-reliability.title: Replication Health and Lag Management
db-reliability.description: Monitor and manage replication lag across PostgreSQL and MySQL replica sets. Implement lag-based alerting and automated corrective actions.
db-reliability.challenge: Create a replication lag monitoring script that queries lag from both PostgreSQL and MySQL replication views, logs to a time-series DB, and fires alerts over 30 seconds.

db-reliability.title: High Availability Architecture
db-reliability.description: Design and deploy high-availability database architectures with automatic failover. Understand the role of VIPs, proxies, and consensus systems in HA setups.
db-reliability.challenge: Deploy Patroni with etcd for a 3-node PostgreSQL cluster, document the failover procedure, and run a chaos test by killing the primary to verify automatic promotion.

db-reliability.title: Incident Response for Databases
db-reliability.description: Build incident response processes specific to database outages and degradations. Write runbooks, conduct blameless postmortems, and improve MTTR over time.
topics: Runbook writing for common database incidents
db-reliability.challenge: Write runbooks for three database incidents (replication lag spike, connection pool exhaustion, disk full), and simulate one incident with a teammate using the runbook.

db-reliability.title: Capacity Planning and Scaling
db-reliability.description: Forecast database growth using historical metrics and plan capacity additions before thresholds are breached. Decide between vertical scaling, read replicas, and sharding.
topics: Disk, CPU, and connection capacity models
db-reliability.challenge: Use 90 days of Prometheus metrics to build a capacity forecast model, identify when disk will be exhausted, and propose a scaling plan with cost estimate.

db-reliability.title: Database Change Management
db-reliability.description: Manage schema and data changes in production safely using migration tools and online DDL tools. Integrate database migrations into CI/CD pipelines.
db-reliability.challenge: Use gh-ost to add a non-null column with a default to a large MySQL table, integrate the migration into a CI/CD pipeline, and implement an automated rollback trigger.

db-reliability.title: Security and Compliance for Databases
db-reliability.description: Implement database security controls required for compliance frameworks including encryption at rest, audit logging, credential rotation, and access reviews.
topics: SOC2 and PCI DSS database controls
db-reliability.challenge: Configure HashiCorp Vault to issue short-lived PostgreSQL credentials, rotate them every 1 hour, and demonstrate that revoked credentials are immediately rejected.

db-reliability.title: Chaos Engineering and DR Validation
db-reliability.description: Use chaos engineering to proactively test database resilience under failure conditions. Conduct regular DR drills and measure actual RTO and RPO against targets.
db-reliability.challenge: Design and execute a DR drill for a Patroni PostgreSQL cluster, measure actual RTO and RPO, compare to targets, and produce an improvement plan for any gaps.

redis-engineer.title: Redis Fundamentals
redis-engineer.description: Install Redis and learn the basics of interacting with it using redis-cli. Understand the key-value model, TTL-based expiration, and the most common data types.
redis-engineer.challenge: Use redis-cli to store session data for 100 simulated users with TTL expiry, and write a script that checks how many sessions are still active after 5 minutes.

redis-engineer.title: Redis Data Structures
redis-engineer.description: Master all major Redis data structures and understand the use cases each is best suited for. Practice building real-world patterns with lists, sets, hashes, and sorted sets.
redis-engineer.challenge: Build a leaderboard for a gaming app using sorted sets that supports adding scores, retrieving top 10 players, and looking up a specific player's rank.

redis-engineer.title: Caching Patterns
redis-engineer.description: Implement common caching patterns using Redis to reduce database load and improve application latency. Understand cache invalidation strategies and cache stampede prevention.
redis-engineer.challenge: Add Redis caching to a REST API using cache-aside, implement probabilistic early expiration to prevent stampede, and measure p99 latency before and after.

redis-engineer.title: Redis Persistence
redis-engineer.description: Understand and configure Redis persistence options including RDB snapshots and Append-Only File. Choose the right persistence mode based on durability requirements.
redis-engineer.challenge: Configure Redis with AOF persistence using everysec fsync, simulate a crash, restart Redis, and verify all committed data is recovered with no loss.

redis-engineer.title: Pub/Sub and Redis Streams
redis-engineer.description: Build real-time messaging and event processing systems using Redis Pub/Sub and Redis Streams. Use consumer groups for reliable at-least-once message processing.
redis-engineer.challenge: Build an order processing pipeline using Redis Streams where producers emit orders and two consumer groups (fulfillment and analytics) process them independently.

redis-engineer.title: Redis Security and Access Control
redis-engineer.description: Secure Redis with ACL-based user management, TLS for encrypted connections, and network-level restrictions. Audit Redis access and apply least-privilege principles.
redis-engineer.challenge: Create Redis ACL users with read-only, read-write, and admin roles, enable TLS, and verify that each user can only perform the commands matching their role.

redis-engineer.title: Redis Replication and Sentinel
redis-engineer.description: Configure Redis replication for data redundancy and set up Redis Sentinel for automatic failover and service discovery. Monitor replication health.
redis-engineer.challenge: Deploy a Redis primary with two replicas and a 3-sentinel setup, kill the primary, and verify Sentinel promotes a replica and clients reconnect automatically.

redis-engineer.title: Redis Cluster
redis-engineer.description: Deploy and manage a Redis Cluster for horizontal scaling across multiple nodes. Understand hash slot distribution, resharding, and how clients interact with cluster topology.
redis-engineer.challenge: Set up a 6-node Redis Cluster (3 primary, 3 replica), distribute a keyspace across it, simulate a primary node failure, and verify automatic failover.

redis-engineer.title: Redis Modules and Advanced Features
redis-engineer.description: Extend Redis functionality using official modules for full-text search, JSON storage, time-series data, and probabilistic data structures. Write Lua scripts for atomic operations.
redis-engineer.challenge: Build a product catalog using RedisJSON for storage and RedisSearch for full-text and faceted search, serving sub-millisecond query responses.

redis-engineer.title: Production Redis Operations
redis-engineer.description: Operate Redis in production by tuning memory, eviction policies, and connection settings. Monitor with Prometheus and run capacity planning using redis-benchmark.
redis-engineer.challenge: Run redis-benchmark to profile a Redis Cluster under load, tune maxmemory with allkeys-lru eviction, and build a Grafana dashboard tracking eviction rate and memory fragmentation ratio.

cassandra-engineer.title: Cassandra Architecture Fundamentals
cassandra-engineer.description: Learn Cassandra's peer-to-peer distributed architecture and how it achieves high availability without a single point of failure. Install a local cluster and use cqlsh for basic operations.
cassandra-engineer.challenge: Set up a 3-node Cassandra cluster locally, verify ring status with nodetool, create a keyspace, and insert and query data using cqlsh.

cassandra-engineer.title: CQL and Data Types
cassandra-engineer.description: Learn the Cassandra Query Language in depth including all data types, collection types, user-defined types, and counter tables.
topics: CQL data types (text, uuid, timeuuid, etc.)
cassandra-engineer.challenge: Design a user profile table using UDTs for addresses and collections for tags, insert sample data, and demonstrate updating individual collection elements.

cassandra-engineer.title: Data Modeling in Cassandra
cassandra-engineer.description: Master Cassandra's query-first data modeling approach by designing partition keys and clustering columns around application access patterns. Embrace denormalization.
cassandra-engineer.challenge: Model a social media timeline for a platform with millions of users, supporting per-user feed queries ordered by time, with no hot partition risk.

cassandra-engineer.title: Replication and Consistency
cassandra-engineer.description: Configure replication strategies and consistency levels to balance between availability and consistency. Understand how quorum reads and writes provide tunable guarantees.
cassandra-engineer.challenge: Deploy a multi-datacenter Cassandra setup, write data at LOCAL_QUORUM, simulate a datacenter failure, and verify reads succeed from the surviving datacenter.

cassandra-engineer.title: Operations and Maintenance
cassandra-engineer.description: Perform routine Cassandra cluster operations including compaction, repair, and node replacement. Understand how to add and decommission nodes safely.
cassandra-engineer.challenge: Add a 4th node to a 3-node cluster, monitor token rebalancing with nodetool, run a full repair, and then safely decommission the added node.

cassandra-engineer.title: Performance Tuning
cassandra-engineer.description: Tune Cassandra performance by optimizing JVM settings, compaction strategies, and caching. Use cassandra-stress and query tracing to identify and fix bottlenecks.
cassandra-engineer.challenge: Run cassandra-stress on a test table, identify the bottleneck using thread pool metrics, tune compaction and caching, and show at least 20% throughput improvement.

cassandra-engineer.title: Security in Cassandra
cassandra-engineer.description: Secure Cassandra clusters with authentication, role-based authorization, and encrypted inter-node and client-node communications using TLS.
cassandra-engineer.challenge: Enable authentication, create roles with table-level permissions, configure TLS for both client and internode traffic, and verify encrypted connections in logs.

cassandra-engineer.title: Monitoring and Observability
cassandra-engineer.description: Monitor Cassandra clusters using JMX metrics exposed to Prometheus via the JMX exporter. Build Grafana dashboards for key Cassandra health indicators.
topics: Alerting on tombstone count and GC pause
cassandra-engineer.challenge: Set up Prometheus with JMX exporter for a Cassandra cluster, build a Grafana dashboard, and create alerts for P99 read latency exceeding 10ms and GC pause over 500ms.

cassandra-engineer.title: Multi-datacenter and Global Deployments
cassandra-engineer.description: Deploy Cassandra across multiple datacenters for geographic distribution and disaster recovery. Configure local consistency levels and latency-aware routing in drivers.
cassandra-engineer.challenge: Deploy a 2-datacenter Cassandra cluster with RF=3 in each DC, write using LOCAL_QUORUM, simulate a DC outage, and verify reads continue from the surviving DC.

cassandra-engineer.title: Production Operations and Disaster Recovery
cassandra-engineer.description: Run Cassandra in production on Kubernetes using K8ssandra, automate backups with Medusa, and validate resilience through chaos engineering experiments.
cassandra-engineer.challenge: Deploy Cassandra on Kubernetes via K8ssandra, configure Medusa for nightly backups, inject a pod failure via chaos tooling, and restore from backup to validate the DR process.

vector-db-engineer.title: Embeddings and Vector Representations
vector-db-engineer.description: Understand what vector embeddings are, how they encode semantic meaning, and how they are generated using pre-trained models. Compare embedding model outputs for different inputs.
vector-db-engineer.challenge: Generate embeddings for 1000 text passages using Sentence Transformers, compute pairwise cosine similarity, and find the 5 most semantically similar pairs.

vector-db-engineer.title: Approximate Nearest Neighbor (ANN) Algorithms
vector-db-engineer.description: Learn the algorithms that power fast vector similarity search including HNSW, IVF, and product quantization. Understand the accuracy vs speed trade-offs.
vector-db-engineer.challenge: Use FAISS to build HNSW and IVF indexes over 1 million 768-dimensional vectors, benchmark query time and recall at different efSearch values.

vector-db-engineer.title: pgvector Fundamentals
vector-db-engineer.description: Install the pgvector extension and store, index, and query vector embeddings directly in PostgreSQL. Integrate vector search with relational filtering.
topics: HNSW and IVF indexes in pgvector
Combining vector search with SQL WHERE clauses
vector-db-engineer.challenge: Store 500k product embeddings in pgvector, create an HNSW index, and write a SQL query that finds the 10 most similar products within a specific category and price range.

vector-db-engineer.title: Pinecone and Managed Vector Databases
vector-db-engineer.description: Use Pinecone as a fully managed vector database to store and query embeddings at scale. Understand namespaces, metadata filtering, and the upsert/query API.
vector-db-engineer.challenge: Build a semantic document search system in Pinecone, index 10k documents with metadata, and implement a hybrid search filtering by document category and semantic similarity.

vector-db-engineer.title: RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation) Patterns
vector-db-engineer.description: Build Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) pipelines that combine vector search with large language models. Understand chunking strategies and retrieval quality evaluation.
vector-db-engineer.challenge: Build a RAG system over a PDF document corpus using pgvector, implement sentence-window chunking, and evaluate retrieval quality using a set of test questions.

vector-db-engineer.title: Weaviate and Qdrant
vector-db-engineer.description: Work with Weaviate and Qdrant as alternative vector databases offering different features like native GraphQL querying and payload-based filtering.
vector-db-engineer.challenge: Index the same 10k documents in both Weaviate and Qdrant, run identical semantic queries, and compare query latency, recall, and developer experience.

vector-db-engineer.title: Multi-modal and Hybrid Search
vector-db-engineer.description: Build search systems that combine semantic vector search with traditional keyword BM25 scoring. Extend to multi-modal search combining text and image embeddings.
vector-db-engineer.challenge: Build a multi-modal product search using CLIP embeddings for images and text, serving hybrid BM25 plus vector results ranked by reciprocal rank fusion.

vector-db-engineer.title: Scaling Vector Search
vector-db-engineer.description: Scale vector search to hundreds of millions of vectors by understanding distributed indexing, sharding strategies, and serving architectures for low-latency search.
topics: Serving latency vs index size trade-offs
vector-db-engineer.challenge: Index 10 million vectors across a sharded Qdrant cluster, benchmark QPS at p99 under 50ms, and tune quantization to reduce memory usage by at least 4x.

vector-db-engineer.title: Monitoring and Operational Best Practices
vector-db-engineer.description: Monitor vector database performance and search quality over time. Set up metrics for query latency, indexing throughput, and recall drift as embeddings evolve.
topics: Vector DB metrics (QPS, latency, index size)
vector-db-engineer.challenge: Set up Prometheus and Grafana monitoring for a Qdrant deployment, implement a nightly recall evaluation job using a golden query set, and alert if recall drops below 0.9.

vector-db-engineer.title: Production Vector Search Platform
vector-db-engineer.description: Deploy a production-grade vector search platform on Kubernetes with automated embedding model versioning, index lifecycle management, and continuous quality evaluation.
topics: Index migration strategies for model upgrades
vector-db-engineer.challenge: Deploy a full vector search platform on Kubernetes, automate embedding model upgrades with zero-downtime index rebuilds, and run an A/B test comparing retrieval quality between two embedding models.

graph-db-engineer.title: Graph Database Fundamentals
graph-db-engineer.description: Learn the property graph model used by Neo4j, including nodes, relationships, labels, and properties. Understand why graph databases excel for connected data.
topics: When to use a graph database
graph-db-engineer.challenge: Model a social network in Neo4j with Person nodes and FOLLOWS and FRIENDS_WITH relationships, insert 50 nodes, and query all friends of friends for a given person.

graph-db-engineer.title: Cypher Query Language
graph-db-engineer.description: Master the Cypher query language for reading and writing graph data. Learn pattern matching, filtering, aggregation, and path queries.
graph-db-engineer.challenge: Write 10 progressively complex Cypher queries against the social network including shortest path, mutual friends count, and recommendation of friends-of-friends not yet connected.

graph-db-engineer.title: Graph Data Modeling
graph-db-engineer.description: Design effective graph data models by thinking in terms of entities, relationships, and the queries you need to support. Apply common graph modeling patterns.
graph-db-engineer.challenge: Model an e-commerce recommendation graph with products, categories, customers, and purchase events as intermediate nodes, then write a product recommendation query.

graph-db-engineer.title: Indexes and Query Optimization
graph-db-engineer.description: Create indexes on node properties to speed up lookups and use EXPLAIN and PROFILE to analyze and optimize Cypher query execution plans.
graph-db-engineer.challenge: Identify a slow Cypher query using PROFILE, add appropriate indexes, run PROFILE again, and document the reduction in db hits.

graph-db-engineer.title: Graph Algorithms
graph-db-engineer.description: Use the Neo4j Graph Data Science (GDS) library to run graph algorithms for analytics including centrality, community detection, and path finding.
graph-db-engineer.challenge: Run PageRank and Louvain community detection on a fraud detection graph, identify the top 5 most influential nodes, and visualize community clusters.

graph-db-engineer.title: Neo4j Security and Access Control
graph-db-engineer.description: Secure Neo4j with role-based access control, fine-grained database privileges, and TLS encryption. Manage users and roles using Cypher administration commands.
graph-db-engineer.challenge: Create read-only analyst and read-write app roles in Neo4j, restrict the analyst role to specific labels, enable TLS, and verify privilege enforcement.

graph-db-engineer.title: Neo4j Causal Clustering
graph-db-engineer.description: Deploy Neo4j in a causal cluster configuration for high availability and horizontal read scaling. Understand Raft consensus, bookmarks, and read replica usage.
graph-db-engineer.challenge: Deploy a 3-core Neo4j causal cluster, configure read replicas, write a test workload that uses bookmarks for causal reads, and simulate a core member failure.

graph-db-engineer.title: GQL and Graph Query Standards
graph-db-engineer.description: Learn the ISO GQL standard and how it relates to Cypher. Understand openCypher compatibility and how graph query languages are converging across vendors.
graph-db-engineer.challenge: Rewrite 5 Cypher queries using GQL syntax, identify and document any behavioral differences, and write a compatibility layer that works for both Neo4j and an openCypher-compatible database.

graph-db-engineer.title: Graph Applications and Use Cases
graph-db-engineer.description: Build real-world graph applications for fraud detection, knowledge graphs, and recommendation engines. Integrate Neo4j with Python using the official driver.
graph-db-engineer.challenge: Build a real-time fraud detection API backed by Neo4j that flags accounts connected to known fraudsters within 3 hops, returning results in under 100ms.

graph-db-engineer.title: Production Operations and Performance at Scale
graph-db-engineer.description: Operate Neo4j at production scale on Kubernetes, implement backup and restore with neo4j-admin, and monitor the cluster with Prometheus and Grafana.
graph-db-engineer.challenge: Deploy a Neo4j causal cluster on Kubernetes using Helm, set up automated daily backups with neo4j-admin, create a Grafana dashboard, and perform a full restore drill.
